output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here’s How to Fight Back
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, legal deadlines, and how to send a demand letter in 60 seconds for $19.99.
content_outline: The Frustration: Why so many landlords get away with keeping deposits
Know Your Rights: State deadlines and what the law requires
Legal vs. Illegal Deductions: What your landlord can actually charge for
Your Action Plan: 4 steps to take before going to court
Why a Demand Letter Works: The formal notice that gets landlords to pay
Fast Solution: How to generate your dispute letter in 60 seconds
Conclusion: Reclaim your money without expensive lawyers
key_points: Most US states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 45 days after move-out.
Normal wear and tear cannot be deducted; landlords may only charge for documented damage beyond reasonable use or unpaid rent.
A written security deposit dispute letter is a legally recognized first step that forces accountability and often resolves the issue without small claims court.
Deposits-Back.com lets tenants create a professional, state-specific demand letter in 60 seconds for a flat $19.99 with no signup or subscription.
output.cta: Stop chasing your landlord. Generate your security deposit demand letter in 60 seconds for just $19.99—no signup, no subscription. Get started at Deposits-Back.com.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters who have moved out and are dealing with a landlord withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit.
